title: Biology Foundations
description: This course introduces the **fundamental principles of biology**, the science of life. Students learn what living organisms are, how they are classified, and the core concepts that underpin all of biology.

title: Cell Biology
description: This course studies the **cell**, the fundamental unit of life. Students learn how cells are structured, how they function, how they communicate, and how they divide to support growth, repair, and reproduction.

title: Molecular Biology
description: This course studies **how genetic information is stored, replicated, expressed, and regulated**. It forms the foundation of genetics, biotechnology, genomics, and modern medicine.

title: Genetics
description: This course studies **how traits are inherited, how genes are passed between generations, and how genetic variation shapes living organisms**. It forms the bridge between molecular biology and evolution.

title: Evolution
description: This course studies **how life changes over time**, explaining the origin of biological diversity through natural selection, genetic variation, speciation, and evolutionary processes.

title: Human Physiology
description: This course studies **how the human body functions**. It explains how organs and organ systems work together to maintain life and homeostasis.

title: Microbiology
description: This course studies **microorganisms**—organisms too small to be seen with the naked eye. It covers bacteria, viruses, fungi, protozoa, parasites, and their roles in health, disease, biotechnology, and the environment.

title: Immunology
description: This course studies **the immune system**, how it protects the body from pathogens, how vaccines work, and why immune disorders such as allergies, autoimmune diseases, and immunodeficiencies occur.

title: Ecology
description: This course studies **how organisms interact with each other and their environment**, from individual organisms to the entire biosphere. It explains ecosystems, biodiversity, climate, and conservation.

title: Biotechnology
description: This course studies **the application of biological systems and living organisms to develop technologies and products** for medicine, agriculture, industry, and environmental science.

title: Biochemistry
description: This course studies **the chemical processes of living organisms**. It explains how biomolecules are built, how cells obtain and use energy, and how biochemical pathways regulate life.
